---
title: "Selina Gaye"
---

## Selina Gaye

**Dates:** (Westminster 1839/40-1914)

### Description

contributor to <i>MP</i> (1866-89)

### Biography

Elder daughter of the Rev. Charles Gaye, Rector of St. Matthews, Ipswich, she was author of fiction and non-fiction for children, including translations from Hungarian,  sometimes as S.G. and sometimes as ‘A clergyman’s daughter’.  She may well be the author of all contributions to <i>MP</i> signed S.G..  By the 1871 census she was an authoress living in London, Coleshill St., with an elderly ?aunt;  in 1891 and 1901 in London with brother Howard Gaye, architect, with no servant.

### Letters

- Letter from June 6th 1890
- Letter from May 17th 1890
- Letter from March 6th 1890
- Letter from Jany 11th [1888]

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
